posts.title: From Component Library to Product Speed
posts.excerpt: Design systems only matter when product teams can move faster. Here is the checklist I use to keep a shared component library useful and lightweight in real product delivery.

posts.title: Avoiding Micro-Frontend Friction
posts.excerpt: Most micro-frontend issues are not technical. They are ownership and boundaries problems. This post covers practical rules that reduce integration friction.

posts.title: Web Components in Large Organizations
posts.excerpt: Web Components are a strong fit for platform teams when contracts are stable. I share the constraints and the wins from enterprise adoption.

posts.title: A Performance Budget for Portfolio Sites
posts.excerpt: A personal site does not need a big framework to feel premium. A small performance budget gives clear decisions for scripts, images, and fonts.

posts.title: Developer Experience Without Overengineering
posts.excerpt: DX improves when projects are predictable. I walk through folder conventions, naming decisions, and where to stop abstracting.

posts.title: Shipping Accessible Navigation
posts.excerpt: Accessible navigation is mostly about small details done consistently. Keyboard support, focus states, and meaningful labels are the baseline.

posts.title: Testing Strategy for UI Platforms
posts.excerpt: A UI platform needs confidence across teams. This strategy keeps tests fast while still protecting contract and visual behavior.

posts.title: Leading Through Code Reviews
posts.excerpt: Code review is one of the best leadership tools for senior engineers. This post shows a practical review style that scales with teams.
